text: ARAGHCHI IN OMAN: Iranian FM met Sultan Haitham at Al Barakah Palace in Muscat — first Gulf visit since war began. Sultan stressed dialogue. Araghchi expected to return to Pakistan then Moscow. Oman playing traditional mediator role between Tehran and Washington.
verificationNote: The National, PressTV, Muscat Daily. Confirmed.

text: HOUTHI RED SEA ATTACK: 3 ASBMs launched — 2 hit Panama-flagged tanker MV Andromeda Star (British-owned, carrying Russian oil from Primorsk to India) near Mokha. Minor damage, vessel continuing. 3rd missile landed near MV MAISHA. Houthis continue striking despite coalition presence.
verificationNote: UKMTO via gCaptain, Frontier Post. Confirmed.

text: KHARG ISLAND CRITICAL: Analysts say today April 26 is the critical deadline — blockade threatens permanent oil well damage if storage overflow not resolved. Iran activated 30-year-old VLCC NASHA (~2M barrels) as emergency floating storage. Bessent says 'in a matter of days' storage will be full. MEES editor disagrees: 'weeks rather than days.'
verificationNote: Gulf News, Yahoo/Benzinga, FDD analysis. Timing disputed by MEES.

text: FIRST US CRUDE REACHES JAPAN: 910K barrels arrived Tokyo Bay from Texas via Panama Canal. Tanker loaded March 22 — took 35 days. Japan to release 36M barrels from reserves May 1 (¥540B/$3.4B). Alternative supply routes slowly activating but cannot replace Hormuz volumes.
verificationNote: Nippon.com, Japan Times. Confirmed.

text: TRUMP PHONE TALKS: Says Iran sent revised proposal 'within 10 minutes' of Pakistan trip cancellation. Called it 'much better' but 'not enough.' Suggests talks could continue by phone rather than in-person: 'We're not going to spend 15 hours in airplanes.' War Powers deadline May 1 — 5 days. State Dept quietly published Epic Fury legal memo.
verificationNote: The Hill, CNBC, Just Security. Confirmed.

text: DAY 58 AM ROUNDUP: Markets closed (Saturday). Brent $104.40 (+30.8%), WTI $94.70 (+25.4%) — Fri close. Gas $4.099 AAA (+30.5%). IDF kills 6 more in Lebanon Saturday. Hezbollah strikes APC in Ramyah. Houthis hit Andromeda Star. Araghchi in Oman. Kharg storage critical. US crude arrives Japan. IMO condemns both US and Iran ship seizures.

text: LEBANON ESCALATION: IDF killed 9 across southern Lebanon in 3 strikes — 4 dead in Yahmar al-Shaqif (Nabatieh), 2 dead + 17 wounded in Safad al-Batikh (Bint Jbeil), 3 Hezbollah fighters in vehicle strike. Netanyahu's office confirmed order to 'strike with force.' Overnight IDF hit 4+ locations across Bint Jbeil, Tyre, Nabatieh districts.

text: MARITIME UPDATE: HMS Diamond (UK Type 45) intercepted Houthi ASBM targeting Maersk Yorktown using Sea Viper system — 2nd attack on that vessel (1st was April 9). MSC Veracruz targeted by drones 600+ nm off Aden. USS Pinckney (DDG-91) intercepted M/V Sevan (shadow fleet) in Arabian Sea — 37 ships now redirected under blockade, up from 34.

text: MARKETS RECORD: S&P 500 closed 7,165.8 (+0.8%) and Nasdaq 24,837 (+1.6%) — both new all-time highs on Friday. Gold $4,741/oz near record. Intel surged 23.65%. Despite war, tech sector confidence driving markets. Iranian rial at 1,320,000-1,553,000/USD. Israeli shekel broke below 3.0 ILS/USD for first time since 1995.

text: DIPLOMACY DEAD: Pezeshkian told Pakistan PM Iran 'will not enter imposed negotiations under pressure, threats, or blockade.' Araghchi left Islamabad for Oman → Russia → back to Pakistan. Hegseth called the war 'a gift to the world.' State Dept legal filing confirmed US entered conflict 'at the request of its Israeli ally.' May 1 War Powers deadline in 5 days.

text: IRAN OIL CRISIS: April 26 marks the deadline FDD warned Iran's oil wells face permanent damage from blockade-forced shutdowns. Could eliminate 300K-500K bpd capacity permanently ($9-15B/yr lost revenue). Bessent: 'Kharg island storage full in days.' Rystad: Iran needs $19B+ in energy infrastructure repairs from strike damage alone. 2M jobs lost since February.
verificationNote: CNBC, Gateway Pundit/FDD analysis, The National. All verified.

text: BLOOMBERG HORMUZ: New April 26 analysis describes worsening 'double blockade' — both Iranian mines/boats AND US naval blockade. Only 3-8 ships transiting daily vs 100+ pre-war. 280+ ships waiting offshore. 800+ vessels stranded globally. 40,000+ seafarers trapped. Baker Hughes: Hormuz won't fully reopen until H2 2026. IMO declared both US and Iranian seizures illegal.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, CNBC, Baker Hughes, USNI News. All verified.

text: NAVAL UPDATE: USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) intercepted Iranian-flagged vessel — CENTCOM now at 34 redirections since April 13 blockade. US-sanctioned supertanker 'Yuri' carrying 2M barrels of Iranian crude from Kharg halted near Larak Island — deterred by blockade. 3 carriers in theater: Ford (Red Sea), Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Bush (Indian Ocean). First 3-carrier Middle East deployment since 2003.

This is the first systematic Iranian cyber campaign against US homeland infrastructure since the war began. 60+ hacktivist groups now active.

Iran FM spokesperson: 'No meeting planned with US.' Iran Defense Ministry: US 'looking for face-saving exit.' Witkoff/Kushner departure reportedly pushed to Sunday. Gap between sides widening, not narrowing.

Bessent: Iran must 'shutter production in 2-3 days' as Kharg Island storage nears capacity. $344M crypto frozen. Hengli (China's biggest Iran customer) sanctioned. text: DAY 57 AM REFRESH: USS Rafael Peralta intercepts Iranian vessel — 34 redirected. Supertanker Yuri (2M bbl) halts at Larak. CISA: Iranian APT hitting US water/energy. Araghchi in Islamabad — no direct US talks. Witkoff/Kushner delayed to Sunday. Hegseth: NATO 'free-riding over.' Gold $4,710-4,741. Brent $105.33. S&P 7,165 record. Gas $4.031.

text: TRUMP CANCELS PAKISTAN TRIP: Truth Social — 'I just cancelled the trip of my representatives going to Islamabad. We have all the cards, they have none. Marks second collapse of Islamabad track after April 12.

text: LEBANON CEASEFIRE BREACH: Hezbollah fires 2 rockets + multiple drones at northern Israel — sirens at Manara, Margaliot, Misgav Am. First rocket barrage since April 16 ceasefire. Drone triggers Malkia sirens, IDF loses contact (UAV crashed). Netanyahu's office orders IDF to 'vigorously strike' Hezbollah. IDF targets Radwan Force facilities + locates anti-tank missile cache. Haaretz tally: 15+ killed in Lebanon since ceasefire began.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog, Haaretz, Al Jazeera. All verified.

Material damage only, no casualties. No claim of responsibility. Iraq Interior Ministry denounced, Kuwait raised border alert. Qatar strongly condemned.
